ideas.title: Adapting Hidden Variable Models from Climate Science for Crystal Facet Prediction
ideas.description: The original idea challenges whether common synthesis parameters are sufficient for accurate crystal facet prediction. Drawing an analogy from climate science, where models evolved by incorporating non-intuitive 'hidden variables' (e.g., aerosol chemistry, ocean salinity) to improve predictive accuracy, this research adapts that solution. We will investigate if parameters beyond the standard set are needed for accurate facet prediction in complex environments, such as biological or mixed-solvent systems. This involves systematically probing for and quantifying the influence of potential hidden variables—like trace impurity dynamics, microfluidic shear, or transient chemical gradients—through controlled perturbation experiments and machine learning-assisted sensitivity analysis. The outcome aims to expand the fundamental parameter set required for reliable synthesis design in non-ideal, complex environments.

ideas.title: Apply Bayesian Optimization from Drug Discovery for Intelligent Synthesis Experiment Selection
ideas.description: This idea adapts the Bayesian optimization loop, a proven method from computational drug discovery for navigating high-dimensional chemical spaces, to the problem of selecting synthesis experiments in materials science. In the analogous field, a surrogate model predicts compound activity, and an acquisition function intelligently selects the next batch of compounds to synthesize, balancing exploration and exploitation. Adapted here, the system would use a machine learning model trained on prior synthesis data to predict material outcomes. A Bayesian optimization layer would then analyze the model's uncertainty and predictions to autonomously recommend the specific synthesis parameters for the next round of lab experiments, aiming to optimally improve the model or discover a target material with far fewer trials than a brute-force approach.

ideas.title: Thermodynamically stabilized, strain-managed growth of micron-scale crystalline tilings
ideas.description: This adversarially improved idea shifts the focus from purely kinetic synthesis optimization to the active management of the fundamental thermodynamic constraints that limit domain size. It combines the computational design of inherently lower-strain tiling architectures with a closed-loop growth system that monitors and corrects strain in real time. This approach directly targets the root cause of defect formation and domain fragmentation, enabling the stabilization of larger single-crystalline regions. The result is a robust pathway to achieve micron-scale, defect-free domains of space-tiled materials by making their growth a thermodynamically accessible state.

ideas.title: A Causal & Adversarial Framework to Test the Necessity of Novel Synthesis Descriptors
ideas.description: This research establishes a falsifiable protocol to determine if new synthesis parameters are truly necessary for predictive materials synthesis. The approach begins by using causal inference techniques on large-scale synthesis metadata to propose candidate missing factors. These candidates are then rigorously tested through an adversarial loop: one model attempts to predict outcomes using only established parameters, while a challenger model incorporates the new candidate. The necessity of the new parameter is validated only if it consistently provides irreducible predictive improvement across held-out chemical systems and its effect can be mechanistically rationalized and reproduced in controlled experiments.

ideas.title: Closed-Loop, Self-Correcting Bayesian Optimization for Facet Synthesis with In-Situ Data
ideas.description: This research direction develops a closed-loop Bayesian optimization (BO) framework for nanomaterial facet synthesis. It integrates real-time in-situ characterization data (e.g., spectroscopic signatures of intermediate states) directly into the BO's Gaussian Process surrogate model as it runs. This allows the algorithm to update its predictions and uncertainty estimates on-the-fly, creating a self-correcting search strategy that adapts to the specific reactor conditions and precursor batch variations of each experiment. Consequently, the system converges to optimal synthesis parameters faster and more robustly than static BO or post-hoc model updates, achieving reliable facet control despite real-world process noise.

ideas.title: Adaptive Model Refinement via In-Situ Data Assimilation, Inspired by Weather Forecasting
ideas.description: This idea proposes to solve the challenge of integrating real-time synthesis data by drawing an analogy from Numerical Weather Prediction (NWP). In NWP, models are continuously corrected via data assimilation, where real-time observational data from satellites and stations is fused with model forecasts to adjust parameters and improve future predictions. Adapted to the context of facet regulation in materials synthesis, this involves implementing a data assimilation framework (e.g., using an Ensemble Kalman Filter) that ingests streaming in-situ characterization data (e.g., from spectroscopy). The system would compare the model's predicted facet state with the observed state, calculate corrections to the model's key internal parameters (such as relative surface energies or kinetic coefficients), and update the model in real-time. This creates an adaptive, self-correcting predictive model that becomes more accurate as the synthesis proceeds, directly leveraging live experimental feedback to refine its core mechanisms.

ideas.title: Physics-Constrained Multi-Fidelity Learning for Robust Property Prediction from Surface Structure
ideas.description: This adversarially improved approach moves beyond simple regression from facet descriptors by embedding known physical laws and multi-fidelity data into the learning process. The system uses facet information as a structural scaffold, which is then enriched with computationally inexpensive quantum-mechanical features and subjected to physics-based constraints during training. An adversarial validation scheme actively identifies and prioritizes learning from challenging edge cases where simple correlations break down. This results in a predictive model that is significantly more robust, interpretable, and reliable for novel material surfaces not present in the original training set.

ideas.title: Physics-Constrained Multi-Fidelity Bayesian Optimization with Knowledge Graph Priors
ideas.description: This system overcomes the cold-start problem in materials synthesis optimization by constructing a transferable prior from domain knowledge rather than historical optimization data. A knowledge graph formally encodes relationships between elements, synthesis techniques, and material properties, integrating low-fidelity data sources like DFT simulations and empirical rules. This graph seeds a multi-fidelity Gaussian process model that guides the Bayesian optimization, allowing it to propose high-value experiments from the very first iteration. The result is a sample-efficient search that respects known physical and chemical constraints while exploring the parameter space for optimal facet outcomes.

ideas.title: Adapting Geospatial Land Cover Classification for Facet Morphology Analysis
ideas.description: This idea adapts solutions from remote sensing and geospatial analysis, specifically Object-Based Image Analysis (OBIA) for land cover classification, to the problem of quantifying facet distributions in materials science micrographs. In the analogous field, satellite imagery is segmented into meaningful objects (like forest patches or water bodies) based on spectral, textural, and contextual features, which are then classified using machine learning models to produce quantitative land cover maps. Adapted to this context, a similar pipeline would be developed for TEM/SEM images: first, segmenting the material surface into distinct facet regions based on morphological features like edge orientation, local contrast, and texture patterns. A classifier (e.g., Random Forest or a convolutional neural network) would be trained on expert-labeled facets to automatically identify and map crystallographic facet types (e.g., {100}, {111}). This allows for the automated, high-throughput quantification of facet area distributions, aspect ratios, and boundary relationships, providing robust morphological metrics that can be correlated with material synthesis parameters or functional properties.

ideas.title: Diversity-Constrained Active Learning with Failure-Informed Priors for Synthesis Optimization
ideas.description: This redesigned system embeds active learning within a robust, multi-strategy acquisition framework to prevent myopic optimization. It uses a dual acquisition function that weights both model uncertainty (exploration) and predicted performance (exploitation), while enforcing diversity constraints via a determinantal point process to ensure broad coverage of the synthesis parameter space. Crucially, a secondary classifier, trained on the outcomes (success/failure) of all prior experiments, acts as a viability filter to deprioritize regions with a high predicted probability of synthesis failure, making the loop sample-efficient and resilient to initial biases.

ideas.title: Causally-Filtered In-Situ Data Integration for Robust Facet Regulation
ideas.description: This redesigned approach integrates in-situ characterization data not as direct inputs, but through a causal filtering framework that separates confounding sensor artifacts from genuine process signals. A dual-model system is trained adversarially to force the core predictive model to learn latent representations that can reconstruct the in-situ data only via features causally related to facet evolution. This ensures robustness against sensor noise, drift, and changing environmental conditions. The final model uses these filtered representations to adjust synthesis parameters in real-time, maintaining high predictive accuracy even when raw sensor data degrades or becomes misleading.
